title: 10 possible side effects of Aloe vera (Aloe Vera) if used improperly
intro: Aloe vera, a popular plant known for its healing properties, can have adverse health effects if used improperly. Consuming large amounts of aloe vera latex, the sticky yellow substance found under the leaf's skin, can lead to stomach discomfort, cramps, and diarrhea due to its laxative properties. Prolonged use or excessive consumption can even cause electrolyte imbalances, which may result in heart problems and muscle weakness. Furthermore, skin reactions, including rashes and irritation, can occur from applying aloe vera gel to broken or damaged skin. Pregnant women should avoid aloe vera ingestion, as it may trigger uterine contractions and lead to premature labor. Lastly, aloe vera may interact with certain medications, such as digoxin, diuretics, and laxatives, potentially causing harmful side effects or reducing the medications' effectiveness. Always consult a healthcare professional before using aloe vera, especially for internal use or if you have a medical condition or are taking medication.
